questions.subject: NSEP

options: The electric field at all points on the Y axis has the same direction.
The dipole moment is $0.2 \mu \mathrm{C}-\mathrm{m}$ along +X axis direction.
No work has to be done in bringing a test charge from infinity to the origin.
Electric field at all points on the X axis is along +Y axis.

questions.q: An electric dipole placed in a non-uniform electric field may experience
options: a net force, but no torque.
no net force, but a torque.
a net force and a torque.

Take the electric potential at a point due to a charge $q$ at a distance $r$ from it to be $\mathrm{V}=\frac{1}{4 \pi \varepsilon_0} \frac{q}{r}$. The electric potential at the origin due to the above system of charges is

questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q:
A charged oil (density $880 \mathrm{~kg} \mathrm{~m}^{-3}$ ) drop is held stationary between two parallel horizontal metal plates 6.0 mm apart when a potential difference of $\mathrm{V}=103$ volt is applied between the two plates. When the electric field is switched off, the drop falls. At a certain time the drop is seen to fall a distance of 2.0 mm in 35.7 s and next 1.2 mm in 21.4 s . (The upper plate in the experiment is at higher potential).
Given that the viscosity of air $=1.80 \times 10^{-5} \mathrm{Nsm}^{-2}$ and density of air $=1.29 \mathrm{~kg} \mathrm{~m}^{-3}$
options: The radius of the drop is $\mathrm{a}=7.25 \times 10^{-7} \mathrm{~m}$
The charge on the drop is $\mathrm{q}=8.0 \times 10^{-19} \mathrm{C}$
The terminal velocity of the oil drop, under its free fall, is $5.6 \times 10^{-5} \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$
The oil drop carries 5 excess electrons

questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q:
Statement I: Work done in bringing a charge q from infinity to the center of a uniformly charged non - conducting solid sphere of radius R (with a total charge Q ) is zero.
Statement II: The potential difference between the Centre and the surface of the uniformly charged non - conducting solid sphere of radius R (with a total charge Q ) is $\frac{1}{4 \pi \varepsilon_0} \times \frac{Q}{2 R}$.
options: If statement I is true and statement II is true and also if the statement II is a correct explanation of statement I
If statement I is true and statement II is true but the statement II is a not a correct explanation of statement I
If statement I is true but the statement II is false
If statement I is false but statement II is true

The value of electric field at a point $P$ on $x$ axis at $x=r(r \gg d)$ is given by $E=\frac{1}{4 \pi \varepsilon_0} \frac{a Q}{r^a}\left(H e r e Q=2 q d^2\right)$ Then

questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q: The frequency of the transverse oscillations of a proton (mass M) trapped in a cylindrical relativistic electron beam of circular cross section of radius R and current $I$ is given by [assume that speed v of relativistic electrons $\approx c$ (the speed of light in vacuum) and ignore magnetic effect]

questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q: Three small identical neutral metal balls are at the vertices of an equilateral triangle. The balls are in turn touched to an isolated large charged conducting sphere whose centre is on a line perpendicular to the plane of triangle and passing through its centre. As a result the first and second balls have acquired charges $\mathrm{q}_1$ and $\mathrm{q}_2$ respectively. The charge acquired by the third ball is [Assume that charge and potential of large spherical conductor change insignificantly in charging of the balls and that charges on balls are spherically symmetric]

questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q: A small positively charged ball of mass $m$ is suspended by a long insulating thread of negligible mass. Other positively charged small ball is moved very slowly from a large distance (along horizontal direction) until it is at original position A of first ball. As a result the first ball rises by h to position B such that $\mathrm{h} \ll /$. Choose the correct statement (s)
options: Electrostatic energy of system of charges is 2 mgh .
Total work done on system to bring two balls in their final position is mgh.
Total work done on the system to bring two balls in their final position is 3 mgh .
Work done on system to bring two balls in their final position does not depend on the magnitude of charges explicitly.

questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q: A conducting thick spherical shell of radii a and $\mathrm{b}(\mathrm{b}>\mathrm{a})$ has been charged with uniform surface charge density $-\sigma C / m^2$ on the inner and $+\sigma C / m^2$ on the outer surfaces. Then
options: the net charge on the spherical shell is zero.
the radial electric field outside the shell is $E=\frac{\sigma b^2}{\varepsilon_0 r^2}$
a radial electric field $E=\frac{\sigma\left(b^2-a^2\right)}{\varepsilon_0 r^2}$ exists outside the shell.
there is a net electric charge in the cavity (i.e. in region $\mathrm{r} < $ a) equal to $4 \pi \sigma\left(b^2-a^2\right)$

questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q: A spherical conductor is charged up to a potential of 450 V . The potential outside, at a distance 15 cm from the surface, is 300 V . Then
options: the potential at 15 cm from the center is 900 V
the charge on the conductor is 1.5 nC
the electric field just outside the surface is $150 \mathrm{~N} / \mathrm{C}$
the total electrical energy of the conductor is $U=3.375 \mu J$

questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q: A sphere of radius $R$, is charged with volume charge density $\rho$ such that $\rho \propto r$ ( $r$ is distance from centre). Variation of electric field $E$ with $r$ (For all values of $r: r \leq R$ and $r>R$ ) is best represented by
